data.body:
PASS-1 review — rubric T5c (Security, overlay): NOT-APPLICABLE / PASS — no findings.

This reviews the FOLLOW-ON plan "Reinstate per-pass latency/cost telemetry (coverage.metrics) on the workflow plan-review gate" (re-add det_ms/llm_ms/total_ms/llm_calls that B-RETIRE dropped with run_review). T5c is an overlay that engages ONLY where a plan adds a real security surface in this application's ACTUAL domain — a new endpoint, network exposure, an authn/authz boundary, storage/transmission of sensitive data, PII, or a credential/secret/grant. This plan adds none of those, so per the overlay's gating + ANTI-FP clauses (derive the model from the app's real domain; do not import generic web-app concepts this app lacks), the criterion is not-applicable.

Grounded reasoning (plan text + code):

- (a) Endpoint/path access level — N/A. No endpoint or network path is introduced. The change reconstructs in-memory telemetry inside one gate-dispatch helper (`_attach_plan_review_metrics`, src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py:121-171) from the workflow recorder's existing per-step `duration_ms` timings, then writes `coverage['metrics']` on the already-existing reducer-ignored REVIEW_RESULT sidecar. No new request surface ⇒ no "undeclared access level"; OWASP access-control categories have no surface.

- (b) Encryption at rest/in transit; PII/secret ID; no secret-logging/leakage — N/A. Captured data is operational metrics only: det_ms/llm_ms/total_ms (wall-clock), llm_calls (a derived cost proxy: finder criteria_count + one per succeeded agent step, gate_dispatch.py:156-164), and a constant `claim_path` marker (gate_dispatch.py:165). No PII, credentials, tokens, or sensitive payload is captured, transmitted, or logged. The store is rebar's local append-only git-backed event log — not a network transmission of sensitive data — so there is nothing to encrypt-in-transit and no leakage surface.

- (c) Least-privilege on new credential/role/grant — N/A. No credential, role, or grant is created; the change reuses the existing REVIEW_RESULT write path. No wildcard / admin-for-convenience surface.

- (d) Secret lifecycle / no plaintext secrets in code/IaC/logs — N/A. No secret is introduced, stored, or logged. The only crypto referenced (the claim-path local HMAC verify) is pre-existing and merely NAMED in a constant marker string ("no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)"); this plan handles no keys/secret material. ANTI-FP: that marker string is not a secret.

Affirmations (T5c): the application's actual security boundaries are unchanged by this purely additive, forward-only observability instrumentation — no new surface, no sensitive data, no credential, no secret. Under the overlay gate + ANTI-FP clause, T5c is not-applicable — clean, no findings.
